div.h1: Benchmark Results Template: Scalar vs SSE vs AVX
div.h3: Comparative Throughput Analysis Across Vector Widths

p.em: Hardware: Intel Core i9-9900K @ 2.4GHz Fixed | Memory: DDR4-3200 CL16

div.p: ⚠️ SIMD Warm-up / Setup Overhead For small n (< 16), AVX latency is dominated by:

ul.li: Memory alignment prologue (PEEL loop for 32B boundaries)

div.p: 📊 Asymptotic Memory Bound At n ≥ 4096, performance gains decouple from instruction width. Both paths converge on DDR4 Peak Bandwidth Limit (~25.6 GB/s). Optimization required: Software Prefetching / Cache Tiling.

ul.li: Cold Cache: No (Data hot in L1/L2)
At $n=4$: Scalar and SSE are comparable due to loop overhead.
At $n=64$: AVX's 8-wide throughput overcomes setup costs.
SSE Speedup > 4.0x: Achieved via aggressive loop unrolling (4x2) and dual-port FMA execution.
AVX Speedup > 8.0x: Theoretical peak reached through ILP and reduced instruction count per cache line.
